response.evidence: Female Hispanic or Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to other demographic groups. Asian females (77.45%), White females (75.92%), and Native Hawaiian females (72.73%) all have higher acceptance rates. Among Hispanic or Latino applicants specifically, females have the lowest acceptance rate (66.27%) versus males (64.81%), but this is still below most other racial/ethnic groups. Additionally, Female Hispanic or Latino applicants have lower average loan amounts ($323,059) compared to Asian and White counterparts ($382,636 and $347,813 respectively), suggesting potential disparate treatment in loan sizing. The acceptance rate for your demographic group is 5-11 percentage points below several other groups, which is a statistically significant gap.

Analysis of female Latino applicants shows a 66.27% acceptance rate (Hispanic or Latino category), which is notably lower than comparison groups: Asian females (77.45%), White females (75.92% for Hispanic, 83.78% for non-Hispanic), and Black females (69.47% for Hispanic, 73.01% for non-Hispanic). Among all demographic groups in the dataset, female Latino applicants have one of the lowest acceptance rates. Additionally, female Latino applicants received lower average loan amounts ($323,059) compared to similarly situated groups like White females ($347,813-$394,712) and Asian females ($382,636-$522,172), despite having comparable debt-to-income ratios. This pattern of lower approval rates and lower loan amounts suggests potential systemic discrimination.

Female Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to similar demographic groups. For comparison: Female Non-Hispanic White applicants have 83.78% acceptance, Female Non-Hispanic Asian applicants have 86.49%, and Female Non-Hispanic Black applicants have 73.01%. The 66.27% acceptance rate for Hispanic Females is the lowest among all female groups in the data. This 17+ percentage point gap between Hispanic Females and the highest-performing female groups suggests a potential pattern of disparate treatment in the loan approval process for applicants matching your demographic profile.
